overview: Leaks in pressurized air and gas systems lead to substantial energy waste, environmental pollution, and operating expenses.
Traditional methods of detecting leaks are labor-intensive and sensitive to ambient noise.
This project aims to automate the leak detection process with a network of ultrasonic vibration sensors.
The sensors will be placed on either side of a suspected leak, allowing its location to be precisely determined.
longTermGoals: Create a sensor network and a GUI for control
Reduce energy wasted in industrial facilities
quickPoints: The majority of industrial facilities use compressed air systems in some way.
Our sensors will help automate the labor-intensive leak-detection process.

images.alt: Compressed air line leak sensor setup
images.caption: An example setup of the sensors on a compressed air line
